refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors with telemetry for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and scheduling algorithms to optimize task assignments.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: DP2 depends on data from DP1 (monitoring).
DP3 depends on outputs from DP1 and DP2.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable; data flow is unidirectional from monitoring to control to optimization.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm not adapting well to varying conditions.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of input data and task models.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure modular interfaces between DP1, DP2, and DP3 to minimize coupling impact.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system including vehicle sensors, control software, and optimization services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and tonnage data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task schedules and optimization commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation or Safety Services modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data processing; low diffusion risk.
Hosts sensors and control actuators; moderate diffusion due to embedded control.
Limited role in energy efficiency; mainly remote control and monitoring.
Primarily focused on safety alerts; minimal containment of energy efficiency functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottlenecks if all processing centralized.
Dependency on reliable network links for telemetry and tasking.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider partitioning optimization functions into submodules within NGHE Solution to improve scalability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR decomposition is clear and DP selections logically address each sub-FR with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralizing all energy efficiency logic in NGHE Solution may increase module complexity.
Network dependency introduces risk of data latency or loss.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as primary owner; Vehicle module supports sensing and actuation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and operational metrics collected through NGHE Solution interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to DP and IM layers with explicit interface definitions.

recommended_revisions: Refine modularization within NGHE Solution to reduce complexity.
Define fallback strategies for network failures affecting telemetry and tasking.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy requirements with clear traceability and manageable risks; ready for detailed design and implementation planning.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next design phase.

assumptions: Effective integration between monitoring, control, and optimization functions.
open_risks: Complexity of optimization algorithms affecting performance.
evidence_needed: Validation of energy_per_ton metric under operational conditions.
